mnemonics: HARP: Human-centred AI Research Principles. Focuses on the core principles that guide research in this area (e.g., fairness, accountability, transparency).
keyPoints: The goal of Human-Centred AI (HCAI) research is to develop AI systems that augment and empower human capabilities, not replace them.
Key application areas include AI-assisted decision support, explainable AI (XAI), and fairness-aware machine learning to mitigate bias.
A core research challenge is the alignment problem: ensuring AI systems understand and act in accordance with human values and intentions.
commonMistakes: Assuming automation (replacing the human) is always the goal, rather than augmentation (partnering with the human).
Focusing solely on technical performance metrics (e.g., accuracy) while ignoring human-centric metrics like trust, usability, and perceived fairness.
